summary.intent: Fix toolbar accessibility bugs in PR #701 for the ars-ui repo, address Codex review feedback, and get CI green for merging.
summary.outcome: Two review fixes were committed and pushed: (1) `SetItems` now seeds `focused_index` to the first enabled item when the roving target is missing/invalid, and (2) the re-enable invariant was fixed so a toolbar re-enabled after registration restores the first enabled item as the roving target. Both fixes included unit/proptest tests and spec updates. CI passed and the Codex review thread was resolved.

repo: The repo uses Codex for automated PR review via `@codex review` comment triggers; approval/reaction can land on the trigger comment rather than the PR-level reaction list.
CI includes multiple lanes: fast checks, clippy, unit tests, feature flag matrices (Dioxus), release verification, and coverage — with the longer lanes taking 5-10+ minutes.
The toolbar core intentionally uses `NoEffect` / no DOM refs; adapter live focus (actual DOM focus) is the responsibility of the adapter layer, not the core state machine.
Commit hooks in this repo are quiet until completion and can take significant time.
Spec lives alongside code at `spec/components/layout/toolbar.md` and has a validation step via `cargo xtask spec validate`.

code.finding: `SetItems` is responsible for seeding `focused_index` to the first enabled item; two bug cases were fixed: (1) when the current roving target is missing or invalid on initial set, and (2) when a toolbar is re-enabled after registration.
Proptest invariant for the toolbar state machine was extended to catch missing roving targets after re-enable sequences.
Spec was updated to document that the core exposes roving attrs only (no focus effects); live DOM focus is adapter-owned. Snippet updated to match new `SetItems` seeding behavior.

workflow: Polling CI with `gh pr checks 701 --watch=false` in sleep loops is necessary since the `--watch` flag blocks and the repo has long-running CI lanes.
Resolving Codex inline threads requires the GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation; `gh` CLI alone does not expose this.
Replying to inline review threads uses `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments/{comment_id}/replies`.
When pushing back on a review finding, verifying the spec text first (`rg` against spec files) is the right approach before deciding to accept or push back.

friction: Commit hooks are silent until completion, causing uncertainty about whether commits were hanging or just slow.

Codex review cycle required re-triggering with `@codex review` comment after pushing fixes; the bot's reaction (`eyes`) disappears without a clear approval signal on the PR-level reaction list, requiring polling of issue comments instead.
CI has many long-running lanes (coverage, release, Dioxus feature matrix) requiring multiple multi-minute sleep/poll cycles before a clean green state.
GraphQL thread resolution is not available via `gh` CLI and requires raw `gh api graphql` calls with manually constructed mutation strings.
open_items: The focus-effect request from Codex (programmatic DOM focus from the core on item registration) was intentionally deferred/pushed back as out of scope for the core contract; this may need a follow-up spec clarification or adapter-level implementation.
Coverage CI lane results were not confirmed in the transcript — worth verifying coverage did not regress after the two fixes.
